title: White Extinction: Metaphysical Elements of Contemporary Western Fascism
author: Bhatt

opening_move.description: The Euro-American far-right appears ideologically diverse and incoherent, but this surface diversity conceals a deep metaphysical unity organized around the idea of 'white extinction'. The article diagnoses this hidden ideological infrastructure.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding the metaphysical substrate of contemporary fascism is necessary for grasping why it tends inevitably toward cleansing violence, rather than stopping at populism or cultural nationalism.

rivals_engaged: accounts of far-right as ideologically incoherent or simply populist
narratives that treat contemporary fascism as discontinuous with historical fascism
theoretical_positioning.description: The paper reconstructs the intellectual genealogy of contemporary fascism by tracing its metaphysical sources (Nietzsche, vitalism, fascist anthropology, cosmic destiny), arguing this reveals a coherent logic that existing accounts miss by focusing only on surface politics.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Genealogical-interpretive analysis: close reading of fascist texts (European New Right, US alt-right, historical sources like Evola, Klages, Günther); tracing intellectual transmission across texts and movements.
epistemic_mode.notes: The argument proceeds entirely through textual interpretation of fascist ideological sources. Far-right texts are the primary object of analysis and also the evidence for the argument. This is interpretive analysis with a strong normative-diagnostic conclusion.

case_type.case_name: European New Right and US alt-right ideological texts (Evola, Klages, Günther, De Benoist, Faye, Johnson, Andersen)

engaged_critically: European New Right (De Benoist, Faye, Andersen)

confidence_notes: The paper is a genealogical-interpretive analysis: far-right texts are both the primary object and the evidence. The epistemic mode is normative_with_interpretive_case where the 'case' is a body of political-philosophical texts read as symptomatic of a fascist logic. The normative conclusion (fascism leads remorselessy to cleansing violence) is explicit and strong.
